---
title: "Data Preperation for Models"
author: "Matthew Morgan"
date: "2025-05-16"
output: html_document
---
```{r load packages}
library(lme4)
library(rlang)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(writexl)
library(lifecycle)
library(stringr)
library(psych)
library(ggthemes)
library(RColorBrewer)
```

```{r load data from directory}
data <- read_excel("raw_results_survey_20250605.xlsx")
```

```{r age histogram}
p1 <- ggplot(data, aes(x = `2. What is your current age in years?`, fill = `3. What is your gender?`)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, position = "stack", color = "white") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Male" = "#9ec9c9",
      "Female" = "#7ea1a1",
      "Other" = "#797d7d"  # add a color for "Other"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Age",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Gender",
  )+
    theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 18),    # X-axis title
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 18),    # Y-axis title
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 18),     # X-axis tick labels
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18),     # Y-axis tick labels
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18),    # Legend title
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18)      # Legend items
  )

# Print the plot to check it
print(p1)

# Save the plot at 300 dpi
ggsave(
  filename = "age_histogram.png",
  plot = p1,
  width = 8,       # width in inches
  height = 6,      # height in inches
  dpi = 300
)

# Create ordered age group variable (1 = youngest, 4 = oldest)
data$age_factor <- cut(
  data$`2. What is your current age in years?`,
  breaks = c(17, 29, 44, 59, Inf),
  labels = c(1, 2, 3, 4),  #
  right = TRUE,
  ordered_result = TRUE
)
table(data$age_factor)

# Filter to just Male/Female for more robust analysis
# Filter data to include only Male and Female responses
data_binary <- data[data$`3. What is your gender?` %in% c("Male", "Female"), ]

# Create contingency table for age groups vs gender (Male/Female only)
contingency_table_binary <- table(data_binary$age_factor, data_binary$`3. What is your gender?`)
print(contingency_table_binary)

# Perform chi-square test
chi_square_binary <- chisq.test(contingency_table_binary)
print(chi_square_binary)


# View expected frequencies
print(chi_square_result$expected)

# View residuals to see which cells contribute most to chi-square
print(chi_square_result$residuals)
```

```{r summaries}
table(data$`6. Please click the item that best describes the neighbourhood you live in:`)
table(data$`18. Would you consider providing a blue space (e.g., large nature pond or mini bucket pond) in your garden, allotment or other available outdoor space?`)
table(data$`3. What is your gender?`)

## chi for gender and location (independent)
data$location <- data$`6. Please click the item that best describes the neighbourhood you live in:`
data$gender <- data$`3. What is your gender?`

# Gender × Location (Male/Female only)
data_binary_loc <- data[data$gender %in% c("Male", "Female"), ]
gender_location_table_clean <- table(data_binary_loc$gender, data_binary_loc$location)
print(gender_location_table_clean)

gender_location_chi_clean <- chisq.test(gender_location_table_clean)
print(gender_location_chi_clean)

# Age and location (not indendent)
age_location_table_clean <- table(data$age_factor, data$location)
print(age_location_table_clean)

age_location_chi_clean <- chisq.test(age_location_table_clean)
print(age_location_chi_clean)
residuals(age_location_chi_clean)
```

```{r remove other countries}
# remove people outside of the UK
data <- data[data$`4. Do you currently live in the UK?` != 
             "No", ]
```

```{r visit frequency}
# Define the recoding function
recode_frequency <- function(x) {
  recode_vals <- c("Never" = 0,
                   "Once a year" = 1,
                   "Every 6 months" = 2,
                   "Monthly" = 12,
                   "Weekly" = 52)
  return(as.numeric(recode_vals[x]))
}

# Apply to relevant columns
freq_cols <- c("10.1. Rivers, canals and dykes",
               "10.2. Park lakes",
               "10.3. Coastal areas",
               "10.4. Estuaries",
               "10.5. Marinas")

data[freq_cols] <- lapply(data[freq_cols], recode_frequency)

# Create binary columns for visit or not (1 if freq > 0, else 0)
binary_cols <- paste0(freq_cols, "_binary")
data[binary_cols] <- lapply(data[freq_cols], function(x) ifelse(x > 0, 1, 0))

# Sum frequency visits across all blue spaces
data$blue_space_total_frequency <- rowSums(data[freq_cols], na.rm = TRUE)

# Mean annual visits across the 5 blue space types (0–52 scale)
data$blue_space_mean_frequency <- rowMeans(data[freq_cols], na.rm = TRUE)

# Sum number of blue spaces visited (binary sum)
data$blue_space_num_visited <- rowSums(data[binary_cols], na.rm = TRUE)


# Select the columns you want to summarize
trip_cols <- c(
  "10.1. Rivers, canals and dykes",
  "10.2. Park lakes",
  "10.3. Coastal areas",
  "10.4. Estuaries",
  "10.5. Marinas"
)

# Compute means for each column
trip_means <- sapply(data[trip_cols], mean, na.rm = TRUE)

# Convert to a table/data.frame for nicer display
trip_means_table <- data.frame(
  Location = names(trip_means),
  Mean_Trips = trip_means
)

# put rivers, canals and dykes over two lines
trip_means_table$Location <- sub(
  "Rivers, canals and dykes",
  "Rivers, canals\nand dykes",
  trip_means_table$Location
)

# Print the table
print(trip_means_table)

# Plot

# Remove numeric prefixes from the Location names
trip_means_table$Location <- sub("^\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\s*", "", trip_means_table$Location)

# Create the bar plot
p2 <- ggplot(trip_means_table, aes(x = reorder(Location, -Mean_Trips), y = Mean_Trips)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1, fill = "#9ec9c9", color = "white") +  # <- width added
  scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))) +                   # <- reduces outer whitespace
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "",
    y = "Mean Annual Visits"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 22),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 22),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 22),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 22)
  )

# Print the plot
print(p2)


# Save the plot at 300 dpi
ggsave(
  filename = "mean_trips_barplot.png",
  plot = p2,
  width = 6,       # width in inches
  height = 6,      # height in inches
  dpi = 300
)

# 
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)  # for combining plots

# Ensure both plots have the same theme for consistent text sizes
common_theme <- theme_classic() +
theme(
axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16),
axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16),
axis.title = element_text(size = 18),


# Legend sizing
legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
legend.key.size = unit(0.9, "cm"),
legend.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "cm")
)

# Apply common theme to p1 and p2
p1 <- p1 + common_theme
p2 <- p2 + common_theme + theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# Combine plots vertically
combined_plot <- p1 / p2  # '/' stacks plots vertically

# Print combined plot
print(combined_plot)

# Save combined plot at 300 dpi (square-ish)
ggsave(
  filename = "combined_plot.png",
  plot = combined_plot,
  width = 7,   # width in inches
  height = 10, # taller to accommodate both plots
  dpi = 300
)

#
ggplot(data, aes(x = blue_space_mean_frequency)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 15, fill = "#9ec9c9", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Mean Annual Blue-Space Visit Frequency",
    x = "Mean annual visits per blue-space type",
    y = "Number of respondents"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r agreement values}
data[data == "7. completely Disagree"] <- "7"
data[data == "1. completely Agree"] <- "1"

recode_agreement <- function(x) {
  recode_vals <- c("Strongly disagree" = 1,
                   "Disagree" = 2,
                   "Partially disagree" = 3,
                   "Neutral" = 4,
                   "Partially agree" = 5,
                   "Agree" = 6,
                   "Strongly agree" = 7)
  # Use unnamed to get numeric vector, and replace unknown values with NA
  as.numeric(unname(recode_vals[x]))
}

agreement_cols <- c(
  "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces",
  "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature",
  "12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition",
  "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing",
  "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"
)

data[agreement_cols] <- lapply(data[agreement_cols], recode_agreement)
write.csv(data, "attitude_plot.csv")
```

```{r adjust nci scoring}
# Define the weighted point values
score_lookup <- list(
  "9.1. 1. I always find beauty in nature." = c("1"=0, "2"=1, "3"=2, "4"=3, "5"=5, "6"=9, "7"=15),
  "9.2. 2. I always treat nature with respect." = c("1"=0, "2"=0, "3"=1, "4"=2, "5"=4, "6"=6, "7"=10),
  "9.3. 3. Being in nature makes me very happy." = c("1"=0, "2"=1, "3"=2, "4"=3, "5"=6, "6"=10, "7"=16),
  "9.4. 4. Spending time in nature is very important to me." = c("1"=0, "2"=1, "3"=2, "4"=3, "5"=6, "6"=11, "7"=19),
  "9.5. 5. I find being in nature really amazing." = c("1"=0, "2"=1, "3"=2, "4"=3, "5"=6, "6"=10, "7"=17),
  "9.6. 6. I feel part of nature." = c("1"=0, "2"=1, "3"=2, "4"=4, "5"=7, "6"=13, "7"=23)
)

# create a new column with weighted scores
for (col in names(score_lookup)) {
  weighted_col <- paste0(col, "_score")  # new column name, e.g. "9.1. 1. I always find beauty in nature._score"
  
  # Convert responses to character
  responses <- as.character(data[[col]])
  
  # Map responses to weighted scores
  data[[weighted_col]] <- score_lookup[[col]][responses]
  
  # Convert to numeric
  data[[weighted_col]] <- as.numeric(data[[weighted_col]])
}

# Calculate total NCI score
weighted_cols <- paste0(names(score_lookup), "_score")
data$NCI_score <- rowSums(data[, weighted_cols], na.rm = TRUE)
data$NCI_score_flipped <- 100 - data$NCI_score

#
table(data$NCI_score_flipped)
boxplot(data$NCI_score)
hist(data$NCI_score_flipped)

data$NCI_group <- cut(
  data$NCI_score_flipped,
  breaks = c(-1, 64, 79, 89, 94, 100),
  labels = c("Very Low", "Low", "Moderate", "High", "Very High"),
  right = TRUE
)

#
ggplot(data, aes(x = NCI_group)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "#4C9F70", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Nature Connection Score Groups",
    x = "NCI Score Group (Flipped)",
    y = "Number of Respondents"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r activity summary}
valid_activities <- c(
  "Nature watching",
  "Dog walking",
  "Exercise (e.g., walking, kayaking, swimming)",
  "Fishing",
  "Photography",
  "Feeding wildlife (e.g., ducks, fish)",
  "Pond dipping",
  "Rock pooling"
)

# Count how many valid activities are in each response
data$total_activities <- sapply(data$`13. Do you carry out any of these activities around blue spaces?`, function(x) {
  if (is.na(x) || x == "") return(0)
  if (grepl("don't carry out any of these activities", x, ignore.case = TRUE)) return(0)
  
  # Count how many valid activities are mentioned
  sum(sapply(valid_activities, function(activity) str_detect(x, fixed(activity))))
})

# Check the result
table(data$total_activities)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Define valid activities

valid_activities <- c(
  "Nature watching",
  "Dog walking",
  "Exercise (e.g., walking, kayaking, swimming)",
  "Fishing",
  "Photography",
  "Feeding wildlife (e.g., ducks, fish)",
  "Pond dipping",
  "Rock pooling"
)

# Function to extract activities from responses
extract_activities <- function(response_text) {
  if (is.na(response_text) || response_text == "") return(character(0))
  if (grepl("don't carry out any of these activities", response_text, ignore.case = TRUE)) return(character(0))
  
  activities_present <- valid_activities[sapply(valid_activities, function(activity) {
    str_detect(response_text, fixed(activity))
  })]
  
  return(activities_present)
}


# Build activities dataset

activities_data <- data.frame(
  respondent_id = 1:nrow(data),
  activities_text = data[["13. Do you carry out any of these activities around blue spaces?"]],
  rivers_canals = data[["10.1. Rivers, canals and dykes_binary"]],
  park_lakes = data[["10.2. Park lakes_binary"]],
  coastal = data[["10.3. Coastal areas_binary"]],
  estuaries = data[["10.4. Estuaries_binary"]],
  marinas = data[["10.5. Marinas_binary"]]
)

activities_long <- activities_data %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(activities_list = list(extract_activities(activities_text))) %>%
  unnest_longer(activities_list, values_to = "activity") %>%
  filter(!is.na(activity)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Heatmap data (unweighted)

heatmap_data <- activities_long %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rivers_canals, park_lakes, coastal, estuaries, marinas), 
               names_to = "blue_space_type", 
               values_to = "uses_space") %>%
  filter(uses_space == 1) %>%
  group_by(activity, blue_space_type) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(pct_total = n / sum(n) * 100)   # overall %


# Weighted preferences

person_activity_prefs <- activities_long %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rivers_canals, park_lakes, coastal, estuaries, marinas), 
               names_to = "blue_space_type", 
               values_to = "uses_space") %>%
  filter(uses_space == 1) %>%
  group_by(respondent_id, activity) %>%
  mutate(total_spaces_for_activity = n()) %>%
  group_by(activity, blue_space_type) %>%
  summarise(avg_preference = mean(1/total_spaces_for_activity) * 100, .groups = "drop")


# Difference dataset
difference_data <- heatmap_data %>%
  left_join(person_activity_prefs, by = c("activity", "blue_space_type")) %>%
  mutate(delta = avg_preference - pct_total)


# Plots

# Unweighted heatmap
p1 <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = blue_space_type, y = activity, fill = pct_total)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct_total, 1), "%")), color = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Coastal", "Estuaries", "Marinas", "Park Lakes", "Rivers/Canals")) +
  labs(title = "Unweighted Activity Preferences",
       subtitle = "Percent of respondents doing each activity in each blue space",
       x = "Blue Space Type", 
       y = "Activity",
       fill = "% of Respondents") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

# Weighted heatmap
p2 <- ggplot(person_activity_prefs, aes(x = blue_space_type, y = activity, fill = avg_preference)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(avg_preference, 1), "%")), color = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Coastal", "Estuaries", "Marinas", "Park Lakes", "Rivers/Canals")) +
  labs(title = "Weighted Activity Preferences",
       subtitle = "Percent of activity use apportioned across selected spaces",
       x = "Blue Space Type", 
       y = "Activity",
       fill = "Weighted %") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

# Difference heatmap
p3 <- ggplot(difference_data, aes(x = blue_space_type, y = activity, fill = delta)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(ifelse(delta > 0, "+", ""), round(delta, 1), "%")), 
            color = "white", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "red", mid = "white", high = "green", midpoint = 0) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Coastal", "Estuaries", "Marinas", "Park Lakes", "Rivers/Canals")) +
  labs(title = "Difference (Weighted – Unweighted)",
       subtitle = "Positive = weighted > unweighted, Negative = weighted < unweighted",
       x = "Blue Space Type", 
       y = "Activity",
       fill = "Δ %") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))


# Combine plots
(p1 | p2) / p3

```

```{r activity plot}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)

# Define valid activities
valid_activities <- c(
  "Nature watching",
  "Dog walking",
  "Exercise (e.g., walking, kayaking, swimming)",
  "Fishing",
  "Photography",
  "Feeding wildlife (e.g., ducks, fish)",
  "Pond dipping",
  "Rock pooling"
)

extract_activities <- function(response_text) {
  if (is.na(response_text) || response_text == "") return(character(0))
  if (grepl("don't carry out any of these activities", response_text, ignore.case = TRUE)) return(character(0))
  
  valid_activities[sapply(valid_activities, function(activity) {
    str_detect(response_text, fixed(activity))
  })]
}

# Build dataset
activities_data <- data.frame(
  respondent_id = 1:nrow(data),
  activities_text = data[["13. Do you carry out any of these activities around blue spaces?"]],
  rivers_canals = data[["10.1. Rivers, canals and dykes_binary"]],
  park_lakes = data[["10.2. Park lakes_binary"]],
  coastal = data[["10.3. Coastal areas_binary"]],
  estuaries = data[["10.4. Estuaries_binary"]],
  marinas = data[["10.5. Marinas_binary"]]
)

activities_long <- activities_data %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(activities_list = list(extract_activities(activities_text))) %>%
  unnest_longer(activities_list, values_to = "activity") %>%
  filter(!is.na(activity)) %>%
  ungroup()


# Function to compute contingency stats with odds ratio
compute_activity_assoc <- function(activity_long, space_var) {
  all_respondents <- activities_data$respondent_id
  
  # Binary flag for activity
  activity_flag <- tibble(respondent_id = all_respondents) %>%
    mutate(activity_flag = respondent_id %in% (activity_long %>% pull(respondent_id)))
  
  # Join space selection
  activity_flag <- activity_flag %>%
    left_join(activities_data %>% select(respondent_id, !!space_var), by="respondent_id") %>%
    rename(space_flag = !!space_var)
  
  # Contingency table
  cont_table <- activity_flag %>%
    group_by(activity_flag, space_flag) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups="drop") %>%
    tidyr::complete(activity_flag = c(TRUE,FALSE), space_flag = c(1,0), fill=list(n=0))
  
  # Percent in activity / non-activity
  pct_in_space <- cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==TRUE & cont_table$space_flag==1] / 
                  sum(cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==TRUE]) * 100
  pct_non_activity <- cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==FALSE & cont_table$space_flag==1] / 
                      sum(cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==FALSE]) * 100
  
  # Odds ratio (add 0.5 to avoid zero)
  a <- cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==TRUE & cont_table$space_flag==1] + 0.5
  b <- cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==TRUE & cont_table$space_flag==0] + 0.5
  c <- cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==FALSE & cont_table$space_flag==1] + 0.5
  d <- cont_table$n[cont_table$activity_flag==FALSE & cont_table$space_flag==0] + 0.5
  odds_ratio <- (a/b)/(c/d)
  
  tibble(
    activity = unique(activity_long$activity),
    blue_space = space_var,
    pct_in_space = pct_in_space,
    pct_non_activity = pct_non_activity,
    overrep = pct_in_space - pct_non_activity,
    odds_ratio = odds_ratio
  )
}


# Apply across all activities × spaces
spaces <- c("rivers_canals", "park_lakes", "coastal", "estuaries", "marinas")

results <- bind_rows(
  lapply(valid_activities, function(act) {
    activity_subset <- activities_long %>% filter(activity == act)
    bind_rows(lapply(spaces, function(sp) compute_activity_assoc(activity_subset, sp)))
  })
)

# Heatmap visualization with odds ratio labels
ggplot(results, aes(x = blue_space, y = activity, fill = overrep)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(ifelse(overrep>0,"+",""), round(overrep,1), "%\n(OR=", round(odds_ratio,1), ")")), 
            color="white", size=3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low="black", mid="white", high="red", midpoint=0) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Coastal", "Estuaries", "Marinas", "Park Lakes", "Rivers/Canals")) +
  labs(title="Activity Associations with Blue Spaces",
       subtitle="Overrepresentation (Δ%) and Odds Ratios (OR) for each activity–space pair",
       x="Blue Space Type", y="Activity", fill="Δ vs baseline (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45,hjust=1),
        plot.title = element_text(size=14,face="bold"))

##
results <- results %>%
  mutate(overrep_scaled = ifelse(overrep < 0, overrep * 3, overrep))

activity_plot <- ggplot(results, aes(x = blue_space, y = activity, fill = overrep_scaled)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(ifelse(overrep>0,"+",""), round(overrep,1), "%")), 
            color = "black", size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#9370DB",    # medium purple for negative Δ%
    mid = "#FFFFFF",    # zero
    high = "#1F78B4",   # dark blue for positive Δ%
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(min(results$overrep_scaled, na.rm = TRUE), max(results$overrep_scaled, na.rm = TRUE)),
    space = "Lab"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Coastal", "Estuaries", "Marinas", "Park Lakes", "Rivers/Canals")) +
  labs(
    title = "Activity Associations with Blue Spaces",
    subtitle = "Tile color = Δ% overrepresentation, labels = Δ%",
    x = "Blue Space Type",
    y = "Activity",
    fill = "Δ vs baseline (%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

# Save the plot at 300 dpi
ggsave(
  filename = "activity_heatmap.png",
  plot = activity_plot,
  width = 8,       # width in inches
  height = 6,      # height in inches
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r remove entries}
#data <- data[data$`6. Please click the item that best describes the neighbourhood you live in:` != 
             #"Rural (i.e., mostly countryside with few houses or buildings)", ]

# remove other genders
# data <- data[data$`3. What is your gender?` != 
             "Other", ]
```

```{r provision}
data$provision <- ifelse(
  data$`18. Would you consider providing a blue space (e.g., large nature pond or mini bucket pond) in your garden, allotment or other available outdoor space?` == "I already do",
  "Yes",
  ifelse(
    data$`18. Would you consider providing a blue space (e.g., large nature pond or mini bucket pond) in your garden, allotment or other available outdoor space?` == "I would if I had the space",
    "Provisional_Yes",
    "Maybe_No"
  )
)

# Set factor levels
data$provision <- factor(data$provision, levels = c("Maybe_No", "Provisional_Yes", "Yes"))

table(data$provision)
```

```{r nature connection composite}
#
data$env_charity_member_num <- ifelse(data$`7. Are you a member of an environmental charity or community group? (i.e., RSPB, \"Friends of\" group)` == "Yes", 1, 0)
data$nature_recording_num <- ifelse(data$`8. Do you participate in any form of nature recording? (e.g., via apps iNaturalist, Seek, Merlin or as an amateur naturalist)` == "Yes", 1, 0)

# Scale variables (z-score)
data$NCI_z <- as.numeric(scale(data$NCI_score_flipped))
data$charity_z <- as.numeric(scale(data$env_charity_member_num))
data$recording_z <- as.numeric(scale(data$nature_recording_num))

# Create composite score by summing scaled variables
data$composite_score <- data$NCI_z + data$charity_z + data$recording_z

# Check summary and distribution
summary(data$composite_score)
hist(data$composite_score, breaks = 50, main = "Composite Score Distribution")
table(data$composite_score)

# Define your custom breaks
custom_breaks <- c(-Inf, -1.0, 1.0, Inf)

# Plot histogram
hist(data$composite_score,
     breaks = 50,
     main = "Composite Score Distribution",
     xlab = "Composite Score",
     col = "lightgray",
     border = "white",
     xlim = c(-3, 3))  

# Add vertical lines for breaks
abline(v = custom_breaks, col = c("red", "blue", "blue"), lty = 4, lwd = 2)


ggplot(data, aes(x = composite_score, fill = factor(env_charity_member_num))) + 
  geom_histogram(position="identity", alpha=0.5, bins=0) +
  theme_minimal() + labs(fill="Env Charity Member")

ggplot(data, aes(x = composite_score, fill = factor(nature_recording_num))) + 
  geom_histogram(position = "identity", alpha = 0.5, bins = 40) +
  theme_minimal() + 
  labs(fill = "Nature Recording", x = "Composite Score", y = "Count",
       title = "Composite Score Distribution by Nature Recording Status")

# Adjust -1.5 and 0.5 as needed based on your histogram
data$connection_group <- cut(
  data$composite_score,
  breaks = c(-Inf, -1.0, 1.0, Inf),
  labels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"),
  right = TRUE
)

# Check group counts
table(data$connection_group)

ggplot(data, aes(x = connection_group, y = composite_score, fill = connection_group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Composite Score by Manually Defined Nature Connection Group",
    x = "Nature Connection Group",
    y = "Composite Score"
  ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Pastel1")
```

```{r attitude to blue space}
psych::alpha(data[, c("12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces", "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature", "12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition", "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing", "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces")])

# composite
# Reverse code '12.3'
data$`12.3_reversed` <- 8 - data$`12.3. Blue spaces in the UK are in poor condition`

# reverse 12.3
psych::alpha(data[, c(
  "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces", 
  "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature",
  "12.3_reversed", 
  "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing", 
  "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"
)])

# remove 12.3
psych::alpha(data[, c(
  "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces", 
  "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature",
  "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing", 
  "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"
)])

# Calculate composite mean score (remove negative statement)
data$blue_space_attitude <- rowMeans(data[, c(
  "12.1. Cities should have more blue spaces", 
  "12.2. Blue spaces are important for nature", 
  "12.4. Blue spaces are important for my health and wellbeing", 
  "12.5. We should “rewild” UK blue spaces"
)], na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r remove respondent (optional)}
#data <- data[data$`6. Please click the item that best describes the neighbourhood you live in:` != 
             #"Rural (i.e., mostly countryside with few houses or buildings)", ]
```

```{r model data}
angus <- data[157,]
ol1 <- data[34,]
ol2 <- data[127,]

# remove other genders
model_data <- data[data$`3. What is your gender?` != 
             "Other", ]

# Write to Excel file without modifying column names or row names
write_xlsx(model_data, "model_data.xlsx")
```

```{r postcode preperation}
# Clean and standardize data postcodes
data$postcode_clean <- toupper(trimws(data$`5. What are the first 3 digits of your postcode? (e.g., HU5, YO1).`))
data$postcode_clean <- gsub("^([A-Z0-9]{2,4}).*", "\\1", data$postcode_clean)

# Read and prepare post_codes
post_codes <- read.csv("post_codes.csv")
post_codes$outward_code <- sub(" .*", "", post_codes$postcode)
unique_post_codes <- post_codes[!duplicated(post_codes$outward_code), ]
colnames(unique_post_codes)[colnames(unique_post_codes) == "outward_code"] <- "postcode_clean"

# Fix known postcode errors in data
postcodes <- data$postcode_clean
postcodes <- ifelse(postcodes == "Y01", "YO1", postcodes)
postcodes <- ifelse(postcodes == "YO2", "YO21", postcodes)
postcodes <- ifelse(postcodes == "YO4", "YO41", postcodes)
postcodes <- ifelse(postcodes == "M66", "M6", postcodes)
data$postcode_clean <- postcodes

# Remove rows with HY17 or NA
data_clean <- data[!(data$postcode_clean %in% c("HY17","NO") | is.na(data$postcode_clean)), ]

# Update keys_data after cleaning
keys_data <- data_clean$postcode_clean
keys_unique <- unique_post_codes$postcode_clean

# Check missing postcodes again if needed
missing_in_unique <- setdiff(keys_data, keys_unique)
missing_in_data <- setdiff(keys_unique, keys_data)

# Merge (inner join)
merged_data <- merge(data_clean, unique_post_codes, 
                     by = "postcode_clean",
                     all.x = FALSE)

head(merged_data)

write.csv(merged_data, "survey_map.csv")

```
